original: Murden & Metz are 1st women to reach South Pole overland (on skis)
Harald V becomes King of Norway on the death of his father, Olav V
NASA civilian pilotNeil Armstrongtakes hypersonic research plane  X-15 to 40,690m
Popeye makes 1st appearance, in comic strip "Thimble Theater"
Shari Lewis [Phyllis Hurwitz], American ventriloquist, puppeteer (Lamb Chop), born in The Bronx, New York (d. 1998)
Kipchoge "Kip" Keino, Kenyan athlete (Olympic gold 1,500m 1968, 3k steeplechase 1972), born in Kipsamo, Kenya
US Submarine Nautilus begins 1st nuclear-powered test voyage
Joe Walker takes X-15 to altitude of 82 km
"Cold Sunday" in the United States would see temperatures fall to their lowest levels in over 100 years in numerous cities
Tim Witherspoon beats defending champion Tony Tubbs by majority decision in 15 rounds at The Omni, Atlanta for WBA heavyweight boxing title
Japan unveils plans to build the world’s largest wind farm near the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant
Earliest evidence for a human transport vehicle uncovered, with drag marks made by travois - long pieces of wood loaded with goods and dragged, 22,000 years old at White Sands, New Mexico
Pittsburgh Penguins Alex Nedeljkovic becomes first goalie in NHL history to have a goal and an assist in same game during 5-2 road win over the Buffalo Sabres

LeBron Jamesbecomes youngest player in NBA history to record 20,000 career points during the Miami Heat's 92-75 victory over Golden State; James, 28 years, 17 days, passes Kobe Bryant, 29 years, 122 days
Azharuddin scores second Test century in second Test (v England)
Hermes rocket launched by European Space Agency
Detroit centerSteve Yzermanscores his 500th career NHL goal as the Red Wings beat Colorado, 3-2 at Joe Louis Arena and joins Gordie Howe as the only players to score 500 goals for Detroit
CaptainRobert Scott's expedition arrives at the South Pole, one month after Roald Amundsen
May Gibbs, Australian children's author known for "Snugglepot and Cuddlepie", born in Sydenham, England (d. 1969)
Antonio Prohias, Cuban-American cartoonist for "Mad" magazine (Spy vs. Spy series), born in Cienfuegos, Cuba (d. 1998)
NASA launches Fltsatcom-3
1st cable car patented, by Andrew Smith Hallidie in the US (begins service in 1873)
Mount Nyiragongo erupts in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, displacing an estimated 400,000 people.

American boxer Deontay Wilder wins the WBC heavyweight championship by unanimous decision over Bermane Stiverne at MGM Grand Garden Arena, Las Vegas; Wilder first American to hold a heavyweight title since Shannon Briggs in 2007
CaptainJames Cookbecomes the first to cross Antarctic Circle (66° 33' S)
Czech tennis starIvan Lendlscores a 6–7, 2–6, 7–6, 6–2, 6–4 win over AmericanVitas Gerulaitisto claim his first of 5 ATP Masters Grand Prix titles at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Leonhard Fuchs, German physician and botanist (Notable Commentaries on the History of Plants) for which the flowering plants and color Fuchsia is named after, born in Wemding, Bavaria (d. 1566)
Duane Hanson, American artist, made life-sized realistic sculptures of people, born in Alexandria, Minnesota (d. 1996)
Douglas Wilder, American lawyer and politician, Governor of Virginia (1990-94), 1st African American Governor since Reconstruction, born in Richmond, Virginia
Toini Gustafson, Swedish cross country skier (Olympic gold 5k/10k 1968), born in Suomussalmi, Finland
Andy Kaufman, American comedian and actor (Taxi - "Latka Gravas"), born in New York City (d. 1984)
Billy T. James [William James Te Wehi Taitoko], New Zealand Maori comedian and singer (The Billy T. James Show), born in Cambridge, New Zealand (d. 1991)
Heini Hemmi, Swiss giant slalom (Olympic gold 1976), born in Churwalden, Switzerland
Italian explorerGiovanni da Verrazzano's sets sail aboard French ship La Dauphine to find a passage to China - finds North America instead
1st Dutch female physician Aletta Jacobs opens her office
Swedish diplomat Raoul Wallenberg, credited with saving tens of thousands of Jews from the Nazis, arrested by Soviet secret police in Hungary
Section of Memphis' Highway 51 South renamed Elvis Presley Boulevard

President Clinton honorsLewis and Clark Expedition, posthumously raisesWilliam Clark's rank from Lieutenant to Captain, makes Honorary Sergeant Shoshone female guideSacagawea, and York, enslaved by William Clark, who assisted expedition
Paul Cuffe, African American civil rights activist, merchant and shipbuilder (helped settle Sierra Leone), born on Cuttyhunk Island, Massachusetts (d. 1817)
August Weismann, German biologist, one of the founders of the science of genetics, born in Frankfurt  (d. 1914)
Eugene Augustin Lauste, French inventor (1st sound-on-film recording), born in Montmartre, France (d. 1935)
Glenn Luther Martin, American pioneering aviator (founded what became Lockheed Martin)), born in Macksburg, Iowa (d. 1955)
Nora Kaye, American prima ballerina, born in New York City (d. 1987)
Jewel Plummer Cobb, American biologist and cancer researcher (melanoma), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2017)
Robert Cormier, American author known for "The Chocolate War" and "I Am the Cheese", born in Leominster, Massachusetts (d. 2000)
Eartha Kitt, American singer (Santa Baby), dancer, activist, and actress (Batman - "Catwoman"), born in North, South Carolina (d. 2008)
John Bellairs, American sci-fi author (Chessmen of Doom), born in Marshall, Michigan (d. 1991)
Daniel Charles Brandenstein, American astronaut (STS 8, 51-G, 32, 49), born in Watertown, Wisconsin
Javed Akhtar, Indian lyricist, poet, and scriptwriter, born in Gwalior, India
Super Bowl V, Miami Orange Bowl, Miami, FL: Baltimore Colts beat Dallas Cowboys, 16-13; MVP: Chuck Howley, Dallas, LB
CaptainJames Cook's last notation in Discovery's ship's log

Battle of Cowpens: American revolutionary forces led by Brigadier General Daniel Morgan defeat the British under Lieutenant Colonel Banastre Tarleton in a decisive double envelopment near the town of Cowpens, a turning point in the American reconquest of South Carolina
Modoc warriors defeats the United States Army in the First Battle of the Stronghold, a part of the Modoc War
-17°F (-27°C), Millsboro, Delaware (state record)
1st fully automatic photographic film developing machine patented
United Nations Security Council holds its 1st meeting, at Westminster Central Hall in Westminster, England
Mexico permitsMoses Austinand 300 US families to settle in Texas. After his death his son Stephen leads a colony to Texas.
Martin Luther King Jr.opens the Chicago Campaign, a nonviolent campaign aimed at achieving fair housing practices
Patrick Ewingbecomes first New York player to reach the 15,000-point mark in his NBA career; scores a game-high 34 points in the Knicks' 106-94 win over visiting Minnesota
11th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees:David Bowie; Jefferson Airplane; Little Willie John;Gladys Knightand the Pips;Pink Floyd; The Shirelles; The Velvet Underground;Pete Seeger; and Tom Donahue
